questions.question: The 1947 Partition of India created Pakistan. How many people were displaced and approximately how many died?
options: 10-20 million displaced; 200,000-2 million killed
1 million displaced; 50,000 killed
50 million displaced; 5 million killed
500,000 displaced; 10,000 killed
questions.explanation: The Partition of British India into India and Pakistan (August 1947) caused the largest forced migration in history — 10-20 million people. Communal violence between Hindus, Sikhs, and Muslims killed estimates of 200,000 to 2 million. Muhammad Ali Jinnah became Pakistan's first Governor-General.
questions.memory_hook: Partition 1947: 10-20M displaced, up to 2M killed in communal violence. Largest forced migration in history.

questions.question: Paleontology studies fossils. What is the process by which fossils are formed?
options: Rapid burial prevents decay, then minerals replace organic material over time
Freezing preserves organisms unchanged
Volcanic ash bakes organisms into permanent casts
Heat and pressure compress organisms into stone
questions.answer: Rapid burial prevents decay, then minerals replace organic material over time
questions.explanation: Fossils form when organisms die and are quickly buried (preventing decomposition), then minerals gradually replace the original organic material. Mary Anning found crucial 19th-century sea creature fossils. The first T. rex was found in 1902.
questions.memory_hook: Fossil formation: rapid burial prevents rot → minerals replace organic material. Mary Anning found sea monsters; T. rex found 1902.

questions.question: Argentina's football team is nicknamed 'La Albiceleste.' What does that mean?
options: The white and sky blue (describing the team's colors)
The white eagles
The silver and blue
The sky warriors
questions.answer: The white and sky blue (describing the team's colors)
questions.explanation: 'La Albiceleste' = 'the white and sky blue' — describing Argentina's jersey colors. They won the 2021 Copa América and 2022 World Cup. The 2022 World Cup Final against France (3-3, Argentina 4-2 on penalties) is considered the greatest sporting event ever by many.
questions.memory_hook: La Albiceleste = white and sky blue (Argentina's colors). Won Copa 2021 + World Cup 2022. Greatest final ever vs. France.

questions.question: Batman (1989): what was the controversy around Michael Keaton's casting?
options: Fans protested that 'Mr. Mom' (a comedy star) couldn't play Batman — but the film grossed $411 million
Jack Nicholson refused to act with Keaton
Tim Burton wanted another director for the film
Keaton wore a blue costume instead of black in early test footage
questions.answer: Fans protested that 'Mr. Mom' (a comedy star) couldn't play Batman — but the film grossed $411 million
questions.explanation: When Michael Keaton was cast as Batman, fans protested heavily — he was known for comedies (Mr. Mom, Beetlejuice). Jack Nicholson received top billing over Keaton. The film proved doubters wrong, grossing $411 million. Prince's soundtrack was also commercially successful.
questions.memory_hook: Batman 1989: fans protested Keaton ('Mr. Mom can't play Batman!'). But it grossed $411M. Nicholson got top billing.

questions.question: Nigeria is Africa's most populous nation with 220+ million people. What is its economic capital vs. its political capital?
options: Lagos (economic), Abuja (political/official capital)
Abuja (economic), Lagos (political)
Both functions are in Lagos
Kano (economic), Abuja (political)
questions.explanation: Abuja became Nigeria's official capital in 1991. Lagos remains the commercial center — one of Africa's biggest megacities (15M+). Nigeria has 250+ ethnic groups and is Africa's largest economy by nominal GDP.
questions.memory_hook: Nigeria: Lagos = economic hub (15M+ people); Abuja = official capital since 1991. 250+ ethnic groups. 'Giant of Africa.'

questions.question: Brown v. Board of Education (1954) overturned which earlier Supreme Court case?
options: Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) which established 'separate but equal'
Dred Scott v. Sandford (1857)
Marbury v. Madison (1803)
McCulloch v. Maryland (1819)
questions.answer: Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) which established 'separate but equal'
questions.explanation: Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) established the 'separate but equal' doctrine allowing racial segregation. Brown v. Board (1954) ruled this unconstitutional for public schools. Chief Justice Warren wrote the unanimous opinion: 'Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal.'
questions.memory_hook: Brown v. Board 1954: overturned Plessy (1896 'separate but equal'). Warren's unanimous opinion: 'Separate is inherently unequal.'

questions.question: Elon Musk founded SpaceX in 2002 with what explicit goal?
options: To make humanity multi-planetary, primarily by colonizing Mars
To create reusable rockets to reduce satellite launch costs only
To compete with NASA
To create the first commercial space tourism business
questions.answer: To make humanity multi-planetary, primarily by colonizing Mars
questions.explanation: Musk founded SpaceX in 2002 explicitly to make humanity multi-planetary. Starship is designed to carry 100+ people to Mars. He has said he wants to die on Mars — 'just not on impact.'
questions.memory_hook: SpaceX 2002: Musk's goal = colonize Mars. Starship = 100+ people. 'Want to die on Mars — just not on impact.'

questions.question: Who proposed the Big Bang Theory in 1927, and what was ironic about their background?
options: Georges Lemaître — a Catholic priest; the theory explains the origin without requiring God
Albert Einstein; he was an atheist
Edwin Hubble; he was a Protestant minister
Fred Hoyle; he was a devout Catholic
questions.answer: Georges Lemaître — a Catholic priest; the theory explains the origin without requiring God
questions.explanation: Georges Lemaître was both a Belgian Catholic priest and a physicist. He proposed the 'primeval atom' theory in 1927. Fred Hoyle (a skeptic) coined 'Big Bang' derisively on BBC radio in 1949. The CMB discovery (1964) confirmed it.
questions.memory_hook: Big Bang proposed by a Catholic priest (Lemaître) in 1927. Named mockingly by skeptic Fred Hoyle in 1949.

questions.question: The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Atlantic to the Mediterranean and is how wide at its narrowest point?
options: 14 km wide at its narrowest
5 km
50 km
1 km
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar is 14 km at its narrowest (between Morocco and Spain) and about 36 km at its widest. ~100,000 ships per year. Britain controls the Rock of Gibraltar on the north side. Ancient Greeks called these the 'Pillars of Hercules.'
questions.memory_hook: Gibraltar: 14 km at narrowest. 100K ships/year. Britain owns the Rock. 'Pillars of Hercules' to ancient Greeks.

questions.question: What iconic image emerged from the Tiananmen Square massacre of June 4, 1989?
options: 'Tank Man' blocking a column of tanks alone
Students raising a flag over the Square
A burning barricade
A child carrying an injured protester
questions.answer: 'Tank Man' blocking a column of tanks alone
questions.explanation: The Tiananmen Square massacre (June 3-4, 1989) saw Chinese troops open fire on pro-democracy protesters. The image of an unknown man blocking a column of tanks became one of the most famous photographs of the 20th century. The death toll remains unknown — estimates range from hundreds to thousands.
questions.memory_hook: Tiananmen June 4, 1989: 'Tank Man' image. Hundreds to thousands killed. Chinese government never released numbers.

questions.question: Steven Spielberg is the highest-grossing director of all time. What two Academy Awards for Best Director has he won?
options: Schindler's List (1993) and Saving Private Ryan (1998)
Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981) and Schindler's List (1993)
Schindler's List (1993) and Lincoln (2012)
questions.answer: Schindler's List (1993) and Saving Private Ryan (1998)
questions.explanation: Spielberg's two Best Director Oscars came for Schindler's List (1993) and Saving Private Ryan (1998). His films have grossed ~$10 billion. He invented the blockbuster model with Jaws (1975).
questions.memory_hook: Spielberg: 2 Best Director Oscars (Schindler's List 1993 + Saving Private Ryan 1998). ~$10B total gross.

questions.question: What is the difference between a 'breakaway' and a 'penalty shot' in ice hockey?
options: Breakaway = open chance with no defenders; penalty shot = awarded when a breakaway is illegally stopped
Both are the same thing
Penalty shot requires the goalie to stay at the goal line
Breakaway only counts if the puck crosses the blue line first
questions.answer: Breakaway = open chance with no defenders; penalty shot = awarded when a breakaway is illegally stopped
questions.explanation: A breakaway occurs naturally when an attacker beats all defenders. A penalty shot is awarded by the referee when a defender illegally stops a breakaway (e.g., a late hit from behind or covering the puck in the crease). Both are 1-on-1 against the goalie.
questions.memory_hook: Breakaway: natural 1-on-1. Penalty shot: referee-awarded when a breakaway is illegally stopped.
